title: Turkish Lentil Pilaf
description: A hearty, comforting Turkish-inspired dish made with lentils and spices, perfect for a quick and easy meal. This pilaf is a staple in Turkish cuisine, often served with a side of salad or yogurt.
instructions: Heat the olive oil in a large saucepan over medium heat.
Add the chopped onion and cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1-2 minutes.
Add the lentils, rice, spice blend, salt, and black pepper. Stir to combine.
Add 2 cups of water to the saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils and rice are tender.
Fluff the pilaf with a fork and serve hot.
Optional: garnish with chopped fresh herbs, such as parsley or cilantro.
